      "severity": "high",
      "category": "exception-handling",
      "file": "server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/utils/CredentialHelper.java",
      "line": 119,
      "title": "IOException wrapped in RuntimeException without context",
      "description": "Line 119 catches IOException from JsonSerialization.writeValueAsString() and re-throws as RuntimeException with no message or cause context. This makes debugging credential creation failures difficult and loses the original exception context. The caller cannot distinguish between JSON serialization errors and other runtime failures.",
      "suggestion": "Wrap the IOException with context: throw new RuntimeException(\"Failed to serialize recovery codes for user: \" + user.getUsername(), e);"
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "error-handling-logic",
      "file": "server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/utils/CredentialHelper.java",
      "line": 124,
      "title": "Silent fallback to local storage may mask user storage configuration issues",
      "description": "Lines 124-127: If updateCredential() returns false (user storage credential creation fails), the code silently falls back to creating in local storage without logging the failure reason. This could mask user storage provider errors or misconfiguration, making it difficult to diagnose why credentials aren't being persisted in the expected storage backend.",
      "suggestion": "Log a warning or error before falling back: if (!userStorageCreated) { logger.warnf(\"Failed to create RecoveryCodes credential in user storage for user '%s', falling back to local storage\", user.getUsername()); }"
    }
  ]
}
